info.title: WLTR API
info.description:
## About this API

**WLTR** exposes a **JSON** REST API for calibration analytics and laboratory administration. It covers: laboratories, lab technicians, users, roles, invitations, instruments, calibration run uploads (raw instrument text, parse metadata, compound measurements, and IS response ratios), analytes and raw-name aliases, the **internal-standard catalog** (`GET /api/internal-standards`), calibration levels, **draft calibration group assembly** (`POST /api/calibration-groups`), and versioned method configurations (regression rules, weighting, thresholds, and immutable snapshots for full traceability).

See **Calibration regression and weighting** for **WeightingMode** values (None, 1/x, 1/x²), per-point weights, snapshot vs live config, and future compute validation.

Base path: **`/api`**. Successful responses are usually **`application/json`**; internal-standard summary endpoints also support **`text/csv`** when you pass **`format=csv`** (see **Internal standard response summaries**).

## Authentication

- **JWT Bearer** access tokens are issued by **`POST /api/Auth/login`**.
- Rotate sessions with **`POST /api/Auth/refresh`**.
- Revoke a refresh token (logout) with **`POST /api/Auth/logout`**.
- Password recovery: **`POST /api/Auth/forgot-password`** → **`POST /api/Auth/reset-password`**.
  - `forgot-password` is intentionally non-enumerating — returns 204 for both known and unknown emails.
  - Successful reset revokes all active refresh tokens for that user.
- New users join via one-time invitation links: **`POST /api/Invitations`** (admin creates) → **`POST /api/Auth/accept-invite`** (user accepts).
- Self-service profile: **`GET /api/Auth/me`** (identity + permissions) and **`PUT /api/Auth/me`** (name, qualifications, hire date).
- Self-service password change: **`POST /api/Auth/change-password`** — verifies current password and revokes all active refresh tokens on success.
- Send the access token in the **`Authorization`** header: `Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}`.
- In Swagger UI, click **Authorize**, enter the raw JWT — Swagger adds the `Bearer ` prefix automatically.

## Authorization

All endpoints require authentication by default. Anonymous routes are: `POST /api/Auth/login`, `POST /api/Auth/refresh`, `POST /api/Auth/forgot-password`, `POST /api/Auth/reset-password`, and `POST /api/Auth/accept-invite`.

Endpoints are protected by **permission policies**. The required permission is documented on each operation.

## Permissions reference

| Permission | Value | Held by |
|------------|-------|---------|
| View | `perm.view` | All lab roles (Viewer, Analyst, QA, LabAdmin, SuperAdmin) |
| Lab user management | `perm.users.manage_lab` | LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Lab role management | `perm.roles.manage_lab` | LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Lab configuration edit | `perm.config.edit` | LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Run upload | `perm.runs.upload` | Analyst, LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Run delete | `perm.runs.delete` | Analyst, LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Groups approve | `perm.groups.approve` | QA, LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Lab settings manage | `perm.laboratories.manage` | LabAdmin, SuperAdmin |
| Laboratory create (platform) | `perm.laboratories.create` | SuperAdmin only |
| Platform manage | `perm.platform.manage` | SuperAdmin only |

## Laboratory scope

- Each lab user has exactly one **`LaboratoryId`** claim in the JWT; all data is scoped to that lab.
- **Platform operators** (Super Administrator) often have **no** `LaboratoryId` in the JWT. Endpoints that must resolve a tenant for a **specific resource id** (for example **internal-standard summaries** and **calibration-group readiness**) require an explicit **`laboratoryId`** query parameter that **must equal** that resource's laboratory (derived from the run's or group's instrument). Omitting it returns **400**; a mismatch returns **404**.
- Other endpoints may accept optional `laboratoryId` or infer scope differently — follow each operation's description.
- Cross-lab reads are blocked. Depending on the endpoint:
  - **404 Not Found** — the id does not exist *or* belongs to another laboratory (IDOR protection).
  - **403 Forbidden** — the resource exists but is in another laboratory (explicit when noted on the operation).

## Common HTTP status codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **200** | OK (body varies by resource) |
| **201** | Created — resource id in body; `Location` is usually a GET path (or the canonical resource path, e.g. `POST /api/calibration-groups` → `/api/calibration-groups/{id}`) |
| **204** | No Content — success, no body |
| **400** | Bad Request — validation failure or business rule violation; body is **ProblemDetails** with `detail` |
| **401** | Unauthorized — missing or expired access token |
| **403** | Forbidden — authenticated but insufficient permission, or resource belongs to another lab |
| **404** | Not Found — unknown id or IDOR-masked cross-lab resource |
| **409** | Conflict — optimistic concurrency failure (stale `RowVersion`), or referential integrity; body is **ProblemDetails** |
| **429** | Too Many Requests — rate-limited auth endpoints |

## Errors

Failures return **RFC 7807 ProblemDetails** (`application/problem+json`) with `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`. Validation errors from FluentValidation include field-level messages.

## Optimistic concurrency

Entities with a `rowVersion` field use optimistic concurrency (e.g. technician profiles via `PUT /api/Auth/me`, instruments via `PUT /api/instruments/{id}`). Fetch `rowVersion` from the last **GET** detail response and supply it in the **PUT** body. A **409 Conflict** is returned when the row was modified by another request between your read and write.

## Data immutability

Some records are immutable by design:

- `CalibrationRun.rawText` and its `textHash` are **never updated** after creation.
- `MethodConfigSnapshot` rows are **append-only** — never edited or deleted; use `GET snapshots/{version}` to read historical configurations.
- Approved `CalibrationGroup` records are **locked** — no further edits to the group, runs, points, or curves.

## Pagination (list endpoints)

These **GET** routes return a **paged envelope** (not a bare array):

- `GET /api/instruments`
- `GET /api/runs`
- `GET /api/Laboratories`
- `GET /api/LabTechnicians`
- `GET /api/Users`
- `GET /api/Roles`
- `GET /api/analytes`
- `GET /api/calibration-levels`
- `GET /api/calibration-groups`
- `GET /api/method-configs`
- `GET /api/method-configs/{id}/snapshots`

Envelope shape:

```json
{ "items": [ ... ], "totalCount": 42, "page": 1, "pageSize": 25 }
```

Query parameters (all optional):

| Parameter | Default | Max | Description |
|-----------|---------|-----|-------------|
| `page` | 1 | — | 1-based page index |
| `pageSize` | 25 | 100 | Items per page |
| `sort` | resource default | — | `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc` |

Supported sort fields by resource:

- **Instruments** — `name` (default, case-insensitive ascending), `createdAt`
  - Optional filters: `isActive` (boolean), `instrumentType` (exact match, case-insensitive after trim)
- **Runs** — `runDate` (default, newest first), `status`, `createdAt`
  - Optional filters: `instrumentId` (GUID), `runType` (`CAL`/`ICV`), `status` (`Valid`/`ValidWithWarnings`/`Invalid`)
- **Laboratories** — `name`, `createdAt`
- **LabTechnicians** — `lastName`, `firstName`, `createdAt`
- **Users** — `email`, `laboratoryId`
- **Roles** — `name`
- **Analytes** — `name`, `createdAt`
- **Calibration levels** — `sortOrder` (default), `levelName`, `trueConcentration`, `createdAt`
- **Calibration groups** — `createdAt` (default, newest first), `status`
  - Optional filter: `laboratoryId` (platform operators only)
- **Method configs** — `name` (default), `createdAt`, `version`

## Bare JSON array list endpoints

These **GET** routes return a **JSON array** in the response body (not the paged envelope above):

- **`GET /api/internal-standards`** — internal standard rows for the **`LaboratoryId`** in the JWT (`id`, `name`, `casNumber`), sorted by `name`. Requires **`perm.view`**.
- **`GET /api/internal-standards/{id}`**, **`POST /api/internal-standards`**, **`PUT /api/internal-standards/{id}`**, **`DELETE /api/internal-standards/{id}`** — see **Internal standards catalog**; writes require **`perm.config.edit`**.
- **`GET /api/runs/{id}/internal-standard-summaries`** and **`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/internal-standard-summaries`** — aggregated IS **response** statistics (JSON or CSV). Requires **`perm.view`**. See **Internal standard response summaries**.
- **`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/readiness`** — regression **readiness** (`isReady`, `blockingIssues`, `warnings`). Requires **`perm.view`**. See **Calibration group readiness (pre-compute gate)**.

## Internal standards catalog

Internal standards are reference compounds used to normalize analyte responses on uploaded runs (**response ratio** = analyte response ÷ IS response when linkage succeeds).

### Operations

- **`GET /api/internal-standards`** — list all internal standards for the JWT laboratory (sorted JSON array). **Permission:** `perm.view`.
- **`GET /api/internal-standards/{id}`** — detail for one row in the caller's laboratory, or **404** if unknown or other-lab id. **Permission:** `perm.view`.
- **`POST /api/internal-standards`** — create (`name`, optional `casNumber`). **Permission:** `perm.config.edit`.
- **`PUT /api/internal-standards/{id}`** — update. **Permission:** `perm.config.edit`.
- **`DELETE /api/internal-standards/{id}`** — soft-delete when no analyte uses it as default IS; **409** if still referenced. **Permission:** `perm.config.edit`.
- **Laboratory claim:** list requires **`LaboratoryId`** in the JWT; if missing, **400 Bad Request** on list (ProblemDetails: *No laboratory context available*) — not an empty array.
- **403 Forbidden** when the user lacks the required permission for the verb (e.g. Viewer calling `POST`).

### Laboratory scope

Each **`InternalStandards`** row has a **`laboratoryId`** foreign key. The list endpoint filters by the JWT **`LaboratoryId`**; analytes may only reference internal standards in the **same** laboratory.

### Linking to analytes

When creating or updating an analyte, set optional **`defaultInternalStandardId`** to one of the `id` values from this list (`POST /api/analytes`, `PUT /api/analytes/{id}`).

### Linking to run uploads

After `POST /api/runs`, the server resolves each target measurement's IS using the **name** of the selected `InternalStandard` row and matching it to internal-standard compound rows in the same file (see **Calibration run measurements and response ratios**).

## Method configuration versioning

Each **MethodConfig** has a monotonic **version** number. Every material change to tracked fields automatically appends an immutable **MethodConfigSnapshot**. Tracked fields include: regression type, weighting, force-zero intercept, label mode, correlation / RSE / %diff acceptance bands, point counts, ICV limit **percent**, and optional **internal standard response** bounds (**`internalStandardResponseMin`** / **`internalStandardResponseMax`**) used when flagging **internal-standard summaries** (inclusive bounds on the **mean** IS response per summary row). No-op updates (identical values) do not increment the version.

Calibration groups capture the snapshot at compute time — historical calibration results and frozen IS thresholds always reflect the configuration that was in effect, regardless of subsequent edits to the live config.

Use **`GET /api/method-configs/{id}/snapshots`** to list the full audit history, and **`GET /api/method-configs/{id}/snapshots/{version}`** to retrieve a specific version.

## Calibration regression and weighting

**Method configuration** exposes **`defaultWeightingMode`** on **`POST`/`PUT /api/method-configs`** and on each immutable **`MethodConfigSnapshot`** (same value frozen as **`weightingMode`** in snapshot payloads). At **calibration compute** time, the regression engine must use the snapshot attached to the **calibration group**, not the live method config — so historical curves always match the methodology that was approved for that run.

### `WeightingMode` (integer enum)

Sent and returned as JSON **integers** (Swagger shows the underlying enum names for reference):

| Value | Name | Per included point weight `w` (curve **X** = true concentration) |
|-------|------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| **0** | `None` | `w = 1` (ordinary least squares when all weights are 1) |
| **1** | `InverseX` | `w = 1 / X` |
| **2** | `InverseXSquared` | `w = 1 / (X × X)` |

Inverse modes emphasize fit at **lower** concentrations (larger weight when **X** is small), consistent with common analytical chemistry practice.

### Included vs excluded calibration points

- Weights apply only to points **included** in the regression.
- **Excluded** points do not contribute to weighted sums; their persisted **`weight`** is **0** after compute.
- For **`InverseX`** and **`InverseXSquared`**, **every included** point must have **`X > 0`** (finite). If any included point has **`X ≤ 0`** (or non-finite **X**), compute must fail with **400 Bad Request** (ProblemDetails **`detail`**) — do not produce NaN/Inf weights. **Excluded** points with **`X ≤ 0`** do **not** trigger this rule because they are not weighted.
- For **`None`**, included points may have any finite **X** (including zero or negative) unless a later regression rule forbids it.

### Quadratic regression (`RegressionType.Quadratic`)

When the snapshot regression type is **Quadratic**, the engine fits **ŷ = a·x² + b·x + c** via weighted least squares (3×3 normal-equation Gaussian elimination; no third-party math libraries).

**Coefficient storage on `CalibrationCurve`:**

| JSON field | Role | Symbol |
|------------|------|--------|
| **`quadraticCoefficient`** | x² term | **a** |
| **`slope`** | x term | **b** |
| **`intercept`** | constant | **c** |

`quadraticCoefficient` is **`null`** for non-quadratic models; `slope` and `intercept` retain their usual linear meanings in those models.

**Degrees of freedom:** p = 3 — so **RSE = √(SSE / (n − 3))** where n is the included point count.

**`correlationR` for quadratic** is the Pearson correlation between observed y and predicted ŷ (corr(y, ŷ)) on included weighted points, rather than the x-vs-y correlation which is not meaningful for curved responses.

**Constraints enforced at method-config save time:** `forceZeroIntercept` cannot be `true` when `defaultRegressionType` is `Quadratic` (returns **400**); `minPointsRequired` must be **≥ 3** for `Quadratic` (returns **400**).

**Constraint enforced at compute time:** the normal-equation matrix must be non-singular (requires ≥ 3 included points with distinct x-values); a singular matrix returns **400**.

**Report label:** curve-summary APIs expose a `modelLabel` field, e.g. `"Quadratic (y = ax² + bx + c)"`.

### Non-regression model (`RegressionType.Average`)

When the snapshot’s regression family is **Average** (RSD-style, not a least-squares line), effective weighting is treated as **None** for included points at compute time, regardless of the stored snapshot **`weightingMode`** on the curve record (methodology remains visible on the snapshot).

### Compute endpoint and point diagnostics

**`POST /api/calibration-groups/{id}/compute`** runs the full regression pipeline and transitions the group to `Computed`:

1. Attaches the latest `MethodConfigSnapshot` (freezing methodology).
2. Materialises `CalibrationPoint` rows from parsed measurements and calibration levels.
3. Assigns per-point weights from the frozen snapshot `weightingMode`.
4. Fits the curve via the snapshot `regressionType` (linear, forced-zero, or quadratic).
5. Stamps **`predictedY`**, **`residual`**, and **`percentDiff`** on every point; SSE/RSE/R² on the curve.

After compute, **`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/regression-inputs`** returns the same point layout enriched with **`predictedY`**, **`residual`**, and **`percentDiff`** from the stored `CalibrationPoint` rows. Pre-compute responses return `null` for those three fields.

**Recompute behaviour:** calling compute on an already-`Computed` group clears all existing curves and points before rebuilding; manual include/exclude overrides are **not** preserved across a recompute — inclusion is re-determined from current measurement data. Validate positive **X** for inverse-weighting modes is enforced at compute time (returns **400**).

- Configure defaults via **`defaultWeightingMode`** on method configs; read frozen values from **`GET /api/method-configs/{id}/snapshots/{version}`**.
- Each **calibration curve** stores **`regressionType`**, **`quadraticCoefficient`** (nullable), **`slope`**, **`intercept`**, SSE, RSE, R², and a **`modelLabel`** string (from `CalibrationCurvePrediction.ModelLabel`) for direct use in reports.

## Calibration groups — create draft

A **CalibrationGroup** ties multiple **CAL** runs (one per distinct calibration level) to a single **instrument** and a selected **MethodConfig** (same laboratory). The server stores the group in **`Draft`** status; it may later be computed, then approved, per the broader calibration workflow.

### `POST /api/calibration-groups`

Assembles a new draft group from an explicit list of run ids.

- **Permission:** **`perm.runs.upload`** (same as **`POST /api/runs`**).
- **Prerequisites (HTTP 400 if missing):** JWT must carry a **laboratory** (`LaboratoryId` claim), and the user must have a linked **lab technician** profile in that lab — identical policy to run upload (see run endpoint documentation).
- **Request body (JSON, all primary keys are UUIDs / GUIDs):**

| Field | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **`instrumentId`** | Yes | Instrument that owns this group. Must belong to the caller’s laboratory. |
| **`methodConfigId`** | Yes | Lab-scoped method configuration. Must belong to the **same laboratory** as the instrument. |
| **`calRunIds`** | Yes, non-empty | Array of **CAL** run ids. **Must be distinct** in the request. Each run must: exist; be type **CAL**; use this **`instrumentId`**; not be **`Invalid`** (see **Calibration run status**); and map to a **calibration level** with a true concentration. **No two runs** may share the same **`calibrationLevelId`** in one group. |
| **`icvRunId`** | No | Optional **ICV** run on the same instrument, same rules as CAL for instrument and not **`Invalid`**. Must not duplicate any id in **`calRunIds`**. |

- **Success (201 Created):** Response body is `{ "id": "<group-guid>" }`. **`Location`** is set to **`/api/calibration-groups/{id}`** (path-style URI; a dedicated **GET** group detail may be added in a later release). Content-Type: **`application/json`**.
- **Client validation (400):** FluentValidation may reject before business rules, e.g. empty **`calRunIds`**, duplicate ids in the array, or **`icvRunId`** equal to a **`calRunIds`** entry — often with field-scoped error messages where applicable.
- **Business rules (400, ProblemDetails `detail`):** include unknown instrument/config/run ids, cross-lab instrument or method config, wrong run type, **`Invalid`** run status, missing calibration level on a CAL run, or duplicate calibration level across CAL runs. Cross-lab or missing resources are described without leaking existence where the API returns a generic *not found* or validation message, consistent with other lab-scoped commands.
- **401** — Unauthenticated. **403** — Authenticated but missing **`perm.runs.upload`**.

Related: **`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/readiness`** (pre-compute gate — see **Calibration group readiness (pre-compute gate)** below), **`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/internal-standard-summaries`** (aggregates only **CalRuns**, not the optional **ICV**). See also **Method configuration versioning** and **Calibration run status** in this document.

## Calibration group readiness (pre-compute gate)

**`GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/readiness`** answers whether a draft (or future computed) group has enough valid **CAL**-level data to run regression safely. It is the authoritative check a future **`POST /api/calibration-groups/{id}/compute`** should reuse: do not compute when **`isReady`** is **false** until **`blockingIssues`** is empty.

**Note:** Readiness does not yet enforce **positive curve X** for inverse weighting (1/x, 1/x²); that validation is part of the future compute step — see **Calibration regression and weighting**.

### Scope

- Only **CAL** runs linked to the group (the many-to-many **`CalRuns`** set) are evaluated. The optional **ICV** run on the group is **not** part of this check.
- **Curve X** (true concentration) comes from each CAL run’s **calibration level**, not from export **`calculatedConcentration`** on rows; some measurement warnings (e.g. malformed export concentration) may appear under **`warnings`** without blocking readiness.

### Endpoint

| Method | Path | Permission |
|--------|------|------------|
| **GET** | `/api/calibration-groups/{id}/readiness` | **`perm.view`** |

### Query parameters

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| **`laboratoryId`** | **Yes** for **platform operators** (JWT has no laboratory); **ignored** for normal lab users | Must equal the **instrument’s** `laboratoryId` for the group. Missing for operator → **400** ProblemDetails. Wrong lab → **404** (IDOR-safe). |

### Response body (200 OK, `application/json`)

| JSON property | Type | Description |
|---------------|------|-------------|
| **`calibrationGroupId`** | string (UUID) | Same as path `{id}`. |
| **`isReady`** | boolean | **false** if **`blockingIssues`** has any item; **true** otherwise. |
| **`minPointsRequired`** | integer | Effective minimum number of **CAL runs / levels** from **`MethodConfigSnapshot.minPointsRequired`** when **`methodConfigSnapshotId`** is set on the group, otherwise from the **live** **`MethodConfig`** referenced by the group’s **`methodConfigId`**. |
| **`calibrationLevelCount`** | integer | Count of CAL runs currently linked to the group (same as distinct levels when create rules hold). |
| **`blockingIssues`** | array | Hard failures; each item matches **`RunValidationIssueResponse`** (see **Issue object** below). Must be **empty** before compute. |
| **`warnings`** | array | Non-blocking findings; same shape as **`blockingIssues`**. |

### Issue object (`blockingIssues` / `warnings` items)

Same schema as **`GET /api/runs/{id}/validation`** issues:

| JSON property | Type | Description |
|---------------|------|-------------|
| **`code`** | integer | **`RunValidationIssueCode`** — see table below. |
| **`isError`** | boolean | Original row severity from run validation; **blocking** list entries are still listed under **`blockingIssues`** regardless. |
| **`message`** | string | Human-readable explanation. |
| **`measurementId`** | string (UUID) or null | Persisted measurement when the issue targets a row. |
| **`rawCompoundName`** | string or null | Export compound name when applicable. |
| **`sourceLineNumber`** | integer or null | Parser line hint when known. |
| **`calibrationRunId`** | string (UUID) or null | CAL run scope for whole-run or group-scoped issues. |

### `code` values (`RunValidationIssueCode`)

| Value | Name | Typical use |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **0** | `NonPositiveResponse` | Target response ≤ 0 — blocking for regression. |
| **1** | `MissingAnalyteMapping` | Row not mapped to a canonical analyte — **blocking** for group readiness (even if run status is `ValidWithWarnings`). |
| **2** | `MissingInternalStandard` | Run has **InternalStandard** rows but **`responseRatio`** missing for a target row — **blocking** for readiness. |
| **3** | `MalformedConcentrationOrRatio` | Export concentration / parser issue — usually **warning** for readiness (curve X from levels). |
| **4** | `NonPositiveInternalStandardResponse` | IS response not usable for ratio — blocking. |
| **5** | `InsufficientCalibrationLevels` | **`calibrationLevelCount`** is below **`minPointsRequired`**, or no CAL runs linked. |
| **6** | `CalibrationRunLevelUnresolved` | A CAL run has no **`calibrationLevelId`**. |
| **7** | `DuplicateCalibrationLevelInGroup` | Two+ CAL runs share the same level in the group. |
| **8** | `CalibrationRunReferenceMissing` | A listed run id was not found (defense in depth). |
| **9** | `InvalidCalibrationRunStatus` | Persisted run **`status`** is **`Invalid`**. |
| **10** | `InvalidResponseRatio` | **`responseRatio`** present but not finite or not positive when IS rows exist. |

### Rules evaluated (summary)

1. **Minimum levels:** **`calibrationLevelCount`** ≥ effective **`minPointsRequired`**.
2. **Level mapping:** every linked CAL run has a resolved calibration level; no duplicate level across runs.
3. **Run status:** no linked CAL run in **`Invalid`** status.
4. **Measurements:** run validation is recomputed from persisted rows; **`MissingAnalyteMapping`**, **`MissingInternalStandard`**, **`NonPositiveInternalStandardResponse`**, and other **`isError`** issues are **blocking** for readiness.
5. **IS / ratio:** when the run contains **InternalStandard** compound rows, each **Target**/**Unknown** row with positive **Response** must have a **finite**, **positive** **`responseRatio`** (additional check beyond run validation).

### HTTP status codes (readiness)

| Code | When |
|------|------|
| **200** | Success — body always includes **`isReady`** and issue arrays (may be empty). Not-ready groups still return **200** with **`isReady: false`** and populated **`blockingIssues`**. |
| **400** | Platform operator omitted **`laboratoryId`** (ProblemDetails **`detail`**). |
| **401** | No or invalid bearer token. |
| **403** | Authenticated but missing **`perm.view`**. |
| **404** | Group unknown, instrument missing, or group not in the **effective** laboratory; IDOR-safe. |

### See also

- **`GET /api/runs/{id}/validation`** — per-run issue shape and run-level **`status`**. - **Internal standard response summaries** — IS **Response** aggregates (separate from readiness). - **Method configuration versioning** — snapshot vs live **`minPointsRequired`**.

## Calibration point exclusion and re-inclusion

A **CalibrationPoint** represents one analyte/level data point within a **CalibrationCurve** that is itself part of a **CalibrationGroup**. Points that carry invalid or unreliable data can be **excluded** from regression so the model reflects only accepted calibration evidence. An excluded point retains all stored values (for audit); it is simply not weighted in the regression computation.

### Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description | Permission |
|--------|------|-------------|------------|
| **POST** | `/api/calibration-groups/{id}/points/{pointId}/exclude` | Mark a point as excluded | **`perm.runs.upload`** |
| **DELETE** | `/api/calibration-groups/{id}/points/{pointId}/exclude` | Re-include a previously excluded point | **`perm.runs.upload`** |

Both operations are **idempotent-guarded**: attempting to exclude an already-excluded point, or to re-include an already-included point, returns **409 Conflict**.

### Exclusion request body (`POST .../exclude`)

```json
{ "reason": "ManualExclude", "note": "Outlier confirmed by analyst — co-elution suspected" }
```

| Field | Required | Values | Notes |
|-------|----------|--------|-------|
| **`reason`** | Yes | `ManualExclude`, `PctDiffOutOfRange` | Only user-initiated reasons are accepted here. System reasons (`MissingRatio`, `MissingIS`, `InvalidX`) are set automatically during data-quality checks and cannot be submitted manually. |
| **`note`** | Yes | Non-empty string | Required analyst justification for audit purposes. |

### Exclusion fields stamped on the point

On successful exclusion, the following fields are persisted on `CalibrationPoint`:

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **`isIncluded`** | Set to `false`. |
| **`exclusionReason`** | The submitted `reason` value. |
| **`exclusionNote`** | The submitted `note` value. |
| **`excludedByUserId`** | Identity user id of the acting analyst. |
| **`excludedAt`** | UTC timestamp of the exclusion. |

On successful **re-inclusion**, all five fields are cleared (`isIncluded → true`, others → null / None).

### System auto-exclusion reasons

These reasons are set automatically during data quality evaluation and are **not** accepted as manual inputs:

| Reason | Condition |
|--------|-----------|
| `MissingRatio` | Response or response ratio is null (no IS method). |
| `MissingIS` | Response ratio is null because the internal standard could not be resolved. |
| `InvalidX` | True concentration (curve X) is zero, negative, or non-finite. |

### Group status guard

Both endpoints refuse modifications on groups in **`Approved`** or **`Rejected`** status, returning **409 Conflict** (same rule as `PUT /api/calibration-groups/{id}`). Groups in **`Draft`** or **`Computed`** status can have points excluded or re-included.

### Laboratory scope (IDOR)

The server resolves `{id}` (group) → instrument → laboratory and verifies it matches the caller's JWT `LaboratoryId`. A missing group, a missing point, a point-to-group mismatch, or a cross-lab access all return **404 Not Found** without revealing whether the resource exists in another laboratory.

### HTTP status codes

| Code | When |
|------|------|
| **204** | Operation succeeded; no response body. |
| **400** | Validation failure — `reason` is not a user-submittable value, or `note` is empty / missing. Body is **ProblemDetails**. |
| **401** | Missing or expired access token. |
| **403** | Authenticated but missing **`perm.runs.upload`**. |
| **404** | Group or point not found, or resource is outside the caller's laboratory (IDOR-safe). |
| **409** | Point is already in the target state, the group is locked (**Approved** / **Rejected**), or an optimistic-concurrency conflict. Body is **ProblemDetails** with `detail`. |

### Audit trail

Both operations write to `AuditLog` with action types `CalibrationPointExcluded` and `CalibrationPointIncluded` respectively, recording the `UserId`, `Timestamp`, `EntityId`, `OldValue` (JSON snapshot of prior state), and `NewValue` (JSON snapshot of new state).

## Calibration run status

Each uploaded run has a computed `status` field:

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `Valid` | No measurement validation issues after parse (includes response-ratio / internal-standard checks when applicable) |
| `ValidWithWarnings` | At least one non-blocking measurement issue (e.g. missing analyte mapping, missing ratio with IS rows present, malformed concentration) |
| `Invalid` | No measurements, no usable target rows after validation, or blocking row errors (e.g. non-positive analyte or IS response) |

Even `Invalid` runs are stored (raw text is immutable once uploaded). They appear in the database but cannot be used in calibration groups until the data source is corrected and re-uploaded.

## Run naming

**`POST /api/runs`** accepts an optional **`name`** field (string, max 256 characters) that sets a human-readable display label for the run. When omitted or blank, the server assigns a default of **`"{RunType} {RunDate:yyyy-MM-dd}"`** (e.g. `CAL 2026-04-24` or `ICV 2026-04-24`). The name is returned on every read endpoint:

- `GET /api/runs` — `name` appears on each list item.
- `GET /api/runs/{id}` — `name` in the detail response.
- `GET /api/calibration-groups/candidates` — `name` on each candidate run row, so group builders can identify runs by label.

The name is **not** immutable — it is set once at upload and is not subsequently editable via this API version.

## Run deletion

**`DELETE /api/runs/{id}`** soft-deletes a calibration run. The run is excluded from all subsequent reads but the raw text and SHA-256 hash remain in the database for audit purposes. The audit trail records the run metadata and text hash under action type `CalibrationRunDeleted`.

### Permission

Requires **`perm.runs.delete`** (Analyst, LabAdmin, or equivalent). Authenticated but missing permission → **403 Forbidden**.

### Laboratory scope

Lab users may delete only runs whose instrument belongs to their JWT laboratory. An unknown run id **or** a run from another laboratory both return **404 Not Found** (IDOR-safe).

### Referential guard — 409 Conflict

A run **cannot** be deleted while it is a member of any **CalibrationGroup** (either as a CAL run or as the optional ICV run). Attempting to do so returns **409 Conflict** with a `ProblemDetails` body. Remove the run from all groups first, then delete it.

### HTTP status codes

| Code | When |
|------|------|
| **204** | Soft-deletion succeeded; no response body. |
| **401** | No or invalid bearer token. |
| **403** | Authenticated but missing `perm.runs.delete`. |
| **404** | Run not found or belongs to a different laboratory (IDOR-safe). |
| **409** | Run is a member of one or more calibration groups; `detail` in ProblemDetails names the constraint. |

## Calibration run measurements and response ratios

After **`POST /api/runs`** parses compound rows, the server **computes internal-standard (IS) normalization** for each **Target** and **Unknown** row that has a resolved **`analyteId`**:

- The canonical **`Analyte`** row supplies **`defaultInternalStandardId`** → **`InternalStandard`** name (choose the id from **`GET /api/internal-standards`** when configuring analytes).
- That name is matched (case-insensitive; hyphens, underscores, and extra whitespace ignored) to a compound row in the same run categorized as **`InternalStandard`**.
- When a match is found and the IS row response is **positive**, the measurement stores:
  - **`internalStandardResponse`** — the IS compound row response used as the divisor.
  - **`responseRatio`** — `analyteResponse / internalStandardResponse` (used before regression).
- When the IS response is **zero or negative**, **`internalStandardResponse`** is still stored and **`responseRatio`** remains null; **`GET /api/runs/{id}/validation`** returns a blocking issue with code **`NonPositiveInternalStandardResponse`**.
- When the run contains IS rows but no IS could be linked for a target row (missing analyte IS assignment or name mismatch), **`responseRatio`** stays null and validation may emit **`MissingInternalStandard`** (warning).

Use **`GET /api/runs/{id}/measurements`** to read per-row values (including `internalStandardResponse` and `responseRatio`). Use **`GET /api/runs/{id}/validation`** for structured errors and warnings (`code`, `isError`, `measurementId`, line numbers).

## Internal standard response summaries

These endpoints help analysts verify that **internal standard (IS)** compound responses are **consistent** within one run or across the **CAL** runs bundled in a **calibration group**. They operate on raw **`Response`** values from measurement rows whose **`compoundCategory`** is **`InternalStandard`** (not on per-target `internalStandardResponse` copies). Grouping matches **`GET /api/runs/{id}/measurements`** normalization: compound names are normalized with the same rules as calibration level / IS name matching (case-insensitive; hyphens, underscores, and extra whitespace stripped for the grouping key).

### Endpoints

| Method | Path | Scope |
|--------|------|-------|
| **GET** | `/api/runs/{id}/internal-standard-summaries` | One acquisition (CAL or ICV) |
| **GET** | `/api/calibration-groups/{id}/internal-standard-summaries` | All **CAL** runs linked via the group's many-to-many; **ICV** run is **not** included |

Create the draft group first with **`POST /api/calibration-groups`** — see **Calibration groups — create draft** (requires **`perm.runs.upload`**).

**Permission:** **`perm.view`** on both GET routes above.

### Query parameters

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| **`laboratoryId`** | **Yes** for **platform operators** (no JWT laboratory); **ignored** for normal lab users | Must equal the **instrument's** `laboratoryId` for the run or group. Wrong or missing (operator) → **404** or **400**. |
| **`methodConfigId`** | No | When set, **thresholds** for warnings are read from this config; it must belong to the **effective** laboratory (JWT lab or `laboratoryId`). Invalid id → **404**. When **omitted**, run summaries use **no** threshold flags; group summaries use the group's **snapshot** if **`methodConfigSnapshotId`** is set, else the group's **live** `methodConfig`. |
| **`format`** | No | Omit or any value except `csv` → **JSON** array. **`format=csv`** (case-insensitive) → **`text/csv; charset=utf-8`** file download with a stable header row. |

### Response shape (JSON)

Array of objects (may be empty if the run/group has no IS rows):

| JSON property | Type | Meaning |
|---------------|------|---------|
| **`normalizedKey`** | string | Normalized compound name key used for grouping |
| **`rawCompoundName`** | string | Display name from one representative row (stable ordering by source line, then id) |
| **`min`**, **`max`**, **`mean`** | number | Statistics over **Response** for that key in scope |
| **`count`** | integer | Number of **IS measurement rows** aggregated (not necessarily number of runs if a file has duplicate IS lines) |
| **`distinctCalibrationRunCount`** | integer or null | **Run** endpoint: **null**. **Group** endpoint: distinct `calibrationRunId` count contributing to that key |
| **`thresholdMin`**, **`thresholdMax`** | number or null | Bounds **echoed** from the config used for evaluation (if any) |
| **`isWarning`** | boolean | **True** if **mean** is strictly below **`thresholdMin`** or strictly above **`thresholdMax`** when that bound is set (bounds are **inclusive** — mean equal to a bound is OK) |
| **`warningMessages`** | string[] | Human-readable reasons when **`isWarning`** is true |

### CSV export

Same data as JSON: one header row, then one data row per summary line. Numbers use **invariant** culture. Fields that may contain commas are quoted. Filename hint: `is-summaries-run-{runId}.csv` or `is-summaries-calibration-group-{groupId}.csv`. Empty IS set → **header only**, still **200 OK**.

### HTTP status codes (summaries)

| Code | When |
|------|------|
| **200** | Success — JSON array or CSV body |
| **400** | Platform operator did not supply **`laboratoryId`** (ProblemDetails `detail` explains) |
| **401** | No or invalid bearer token |
| **403** | Authenticated but missing **`perm.view`** |
| **404** | Run/group not found, instrument missing, **or** resource not in the effective laboratory (including bad **`methodConfigId`**); responses are IDOR-safe |

### Configuring thresholds

Set optional **`internalStandardResponseMin`** / **`internalStandardResponseMax`** on **`POST`/`PUT /api/method-configs`**. They are copied into each new **MethodConfigSnapshot** on material change. Snapshot columns drive **group** summaries after compute; draft groups without a snapshot use the **live** config for threshold evaluation.

## Rate limiting

The following routes use a fixed-window rate limiter. Expect **429 Too Many Requests** when the limit is exceeded:

- `POST /api/Auth/login`
- `POST /api/Auth/refresh`
- `POST /api/Auth/forgot-password`
- `POST /api/Auth/reset-password`
- `POST /api/Auth/accept-invite`
- `POST /api/Invitations` (invitation create)

The fitted curve trace is omitted when the regression type is `Average` or fewer than two included points are present. post.summary: Runs weighted least-squares regression for the group, persisting fitted curves and per-point diagnostics.
post.description: Executes the full regression pipeline for every analyte present in the group's CAL runs: Attaches the latest `MethodConfigSnapshot` (freezing methodology at this moment).Materialises `CalibrationPoint` rows from parsed measurements and calibration levels.Assigns per-point weights from the frozen snapshot weighting mode.Fits the curve using the snapshot regression type (linear, forced-zero, or quadratic).Stamps `predictedY`, `residual`, and `percentDiff` onto every point, plus SSE/RSE/R² on the curve. Idempotent recompute: calling this endpoint on an already-`Computed` group clears all existing curves and points before rebuilding. Manual include/exclude overrides are not preserved; inclusion is re-determined by the pipeline from the current measurement data. To change point exclusion after compute, call `POST .../points/{pointId}/exclude` and then recompute. Status transitions: `Draft` and `Computed` groups may be (re)computed. `Approved` or `Rejected` groups are immutable — returns 409 Conflict. Validation: the group must pass the readiness check (use `GET .../readiness`) before calling this endpoint. If regression fails for all analytes, the request returns 400 and the group retains its previous status. If regression fails for a subset of analytes, the successful analytes are persisted and the group transitions to `Computed`. Laboratory scope: lab users may only compute groups whose instrument belongs to their JWT laboratory. Out-of-scope or missing groups return 404.

Group status rules: exclusion is allowed only when the group is in `Draft` or `Computed` status. `Approved` and `Rejected` groups are immutable — returns 409 Conflict. Returns 409 when the point is already excluded (prevents silent overwrite of the exclusion note). Laboratory scope: the point's containing group must belong to the caller's JWT laboratory. Out-of-scope or missing identifiers return 404.

Supported fields: `sortOrder` (default), `levelName`, `trueConcentration`, `createdAt`.

post.summary: Creates a new calibration level for the caller's laboratory.
post.description: The laboratory is derived from the `LaboratoryId` JWT claim; the caller cannot specify a different lab. Returns 400 if there is no laboratory claim in the token. Name uniqueness: the name is normalized (trimmed, lowercased, underscores/hyphens/ whitespace stripped) before the uniqueness check. `Cal_1ppb` and `CAL 1 PPB` collide; the duplicate returns 400 with the conflicting name in the error detail. On success the response body contains the new level's GUID and the `Location` header points to `GET /api/calibration-levels/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Level name, true concentration (≥ 0), and display sort order (≥ 0).

get.summary: Returns full calibration level detail by id.
get.description: Returns 404 if the level does not exist, has been soft-deleted, or belongs to a different laboratory than the caller's JWT claim. Cross-lab reads are not distinguished from not-found to prevent IDOR enumeration.

put.summary: Updates a calibration level. Edits apply to future work only; historical computations are unaffected.
put.description: "Future work only" means that `CalibrationPoint` rows already computed retain the concentration values used at computation time. Only new runs and recomputations will use the updated value. If the name changes, the same normalization and per-laboratory uniqueness check as create is applied; a collision returns 400. Attempting to update a level that belongs to a different laboratory also returns 400 (IDOR protection).

requestBody.description: Replacement level name, true concentration (≥ 0), and display sort order (≥ 0).

delete.summary: Soft-deletes a calibration level. Returns 409 if referenced by any run or computed point.
delete.description: The level is soft-deleted (excluded from all queries) rather than physically removed to preserve audit trail integrity. Returns 409 Conflict if at least one `CalibrationRun` or `CalibrationPoint` references this level. Remove or reassign those records first. Returns 400 if the level is not found, has already been deleted, or belongs to a different laboratory than the caller's JWT claim.

get.summary: Returns a page of instruments visible to the caller.
get.description: Laboratory scope: lab users see only instruments belonging to their JWT laboratory. Platform operators see instruments across all laboratories. Sort: `name` (default, case-insensitive ascending) or `createdAt`; append `:asc` or `:desc` to override direction. Filters: optional isActive (exact) and instrumentType (case-insensitive equality after trim). Omitted or blank filter arguments have no effect.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort expression: `name` or `createdAt`, optionally suffixed with `:asc` or `:desc`.

parameters.name: isActive
parameters.description: When set, only instruments with this active flag are returned.

parameters.name: instrumentType
parameters.description: When set, only instruments whose type equals this value (case-insensitive) are returned.

post.summary: Creates an instrument in the caller's laboratory.
post.description: The laboratory is derived from the `LaboratoryId` JWT claim; callers cannot specify a different lab. Returns 400 when the name is blank, exceeds the maximum length, duplicates an existing instrument name in the same lab (case-insensitive), or when the token has no laboratory claim. Returns 403 for platform operators. On success the response body contains the new instrument GUID and the `Location` header is set to `/api/instruments/{id}`.

requestBody.description: New instrument fields.

get.summary: Returns full detail for a single instrument.
get.description: Laboratory scope: lab users may only access instruments belonging to their JWT laboratory. Platform operators may access any instrument. Out-of-scope or missing instruments return 404 (indistinguishable by design to prevent tenancy inference). The detail response includes all audit fields (`createdAt`, `createdBy`, `updatedAt`, `updatedBy`) and `rowVersion` for optimistic concurrency on `PUT`.

put.summary: Replaces mutable fields on an instrument.
put.description: Returns 404 when the id is unknown or outside the caller's laboratory. Returns 409 when `rowVersion` does not match the stored concurrency token. Returns 400 for validation or duplicate name (case-insensitive within the lab). Returns 403 for platform operators.

requestBody.description: Replacement field values and concurrency token.

delete.summary: Soft-deletes an instrument (excluded from subsequent list/detail queries).
delete.description: Returns 404 when the id is unknown or outside the caller's laboratory. Existing calibration runs and groups retain the instrument id for traceability. Returns 403 for platform operators.

post.summary: Adds an analyte to the instrument's suppression list; the analyte will be skipped when future runs are uploaded.
post.description: Only mapped analytes (those with a canonical record in the caller's lab) can be suppressed. Unmapped compound rows in raw run text are unaffected. Suppression does not affect runs already uploaded; it takes effect on the next upload. Platform operators cannot mutate instrument configuration — returns 403.

requestBody.description: Analyte to suppress and optional note.

get.summary: Returns all analytes suppressed on the specified instrument.
get.description: Laboratory scope: lab users may only read instruments in their JWT laboratory. Out-of-scope or missing instruments return 404.

delete.summary: Removes an analyte from the instrument's suppression list.
delete.description: Future run uploads will resume storing measurements for this analyte. Already-uploaded runs are not affected. Platform operators cannot mutate instrument configuration — returns 403.

get.summary: Returns every internal standard for the caller's laboratory, sorted by name.

post.summary: Creates an internal standard in the caller's laboratory.
post.description: The laboratory is derived from the `LaboratoryId` JWT claim; the caller cannot specify a different lab. Name uniqueness: names are normalized (trimmed, lowercased, hyphens/underscores/whitespace stripped) before the uniqueness check. Duplicates within the same laboratory return 400. On success the response body contains the new internal standard GUID and the `Location` header points to `GET /api/internal-standards/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Name (required) and optional CAS number for the internal standard.

get.summary: Returns one internal standard by id within the caller's laboratory.

put.summary: Updates an internal standard in the caller's laboratory.
put.description: Applies the same name normalization and per-laboratory uniqueness check as create. Returns 400 when the internal standard is not found or belongs to another laboratory (cross-lab ids are treated as not-found to prevent IDOR enumeration).

requestBody.description: Replacement name and optional CAS number.

delete.summary: Soft-deletes an internal standard when no analyte uses it as default IS.
delete.description: The internal standard is soft-deleted (excluded from all queries) to preserve audit trail integrity. Returns 409 Conflict when at least one canonical `Analyte` in the laboratory still references this internal standard via `defaultInternalStandardId`. Update or clear those analyte assignments before deleting. Returns 400 when the internal standard is not found, already deleted, or belongs to another laboratory.

post.summary: Creates a laboratory invitation, sends an acceptance email to the invitee, and returns the one-time raw token.
post.description: On success the invitee receives an email with a link of the form `{Invitation:FrontendBaseUrl}{Invitation:AcceptPath}?token=`. Clicking the link opens the frontend acceptance page with the token pre-populated. The `rawToken` in the response is the same plaintext token embedded in the email link. It is shown once and cannot be retrieved again (only the server-side hash is persisted). In local development, when `AzureEmail:Enabled` is `false`, the link is printed to the console instead of being emailed — use it to test the acceptance flow without ACS. If `initialRoleId` is supplied, the user receives that role on acceptance instead of the default `Viewer` role. The role must belong to the same laboratory (or be a built-in role); cross-lab or unknown role ids return 400. Platform operators can target any laboratory by providing `LaboratoryId`. Lab admins always create invitations within their own lab.

requestBody.description: Invitee email, optional laboratory id, optional validity window (days), and optional initial role id.

get.summary: Returns a page of technician profiles visible to the caller.
get.description:
Lab users see only technicians in their own laboratory.
Platform operators see all technicians across all laboratories.

post.summary: Creates a lab technician profile and links it to an existing Identity user.
post.description: The profile is created within the caller's laboratory scope (from the `LaboratoryId` JWT claim). Platform operators can specify a `LaboratoryId` in the request body to create for any lab. The `IdentityUserId` in the request must refer to an existing Identity user who does not already have a technician profile. Returns 400 when the user is not found, belongs to a different laboratory, or already has a profile. On success the response body contains the new technician GUID and the `Location` header points to `GET /api/LabTechnicians/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Identity user id, first name, last name, optional qualifications and hire date.

get.summary: Returns a technician profile by id, scoped to the caller's laboratory.
get.description: Returns 404 when the profile does not exist or belongs to a different laboratory. Cross-lab ids are not distinguished from not-found to prevent IDOR enumeration.

put.summary: Updates an existing lab technician profile.
put.description: Lab admins can update any technician in their own laboratory; platform operators can update any. Returns 400 when the profile is not found or belongs to another laboratory. This endpoint performs an admin update (name, qualifications, hire date, active flag). For the self-service flow where a user updates their own profile without admin permissions, use `PUT /api/Auth/me`.

requestBody.description: Replacement name, qualifications, hire date, and active flag.

get.summary: Returns the technician profile linked to the currently authenticated user.
get.description: Requires `perm.view` because this returns domain data from the `LabTechnician` table. Returns 404 when the current user does not have a linked technician profile (e.g. platform operators, or lab users who have not yet been given a profile by an admin). For basic identity context (user id, email, roles, permissions) without a `perm.view` requirement, use `GET /api/Auth/me` instead.

get.summary: Returns a page of laboratories visible to the caller.
get.description:
Lab users see only their own laboratory (at most one row).
Platform operators see all laboratories across the system.

post.summary: Creates a new laboratory (platform operators only).
post.description: Requires the `perm.laboratories.create` permission, which is held only by the WLTR Super Administrator. Lab admins and other lab-scoped users cannot create laboratories. After creation, an email notification is dispatched to configured recipients via the laboratory email notifier. The notification is fire-and-forget; failure does not roll back the lab record. On success the response body contains the new laboratory GUID and the `Location` header points to `GET /api/Laboratories/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Laboratory name, address, and optional contact information.

get.summary: Returns full laboratory details by id.
get.description: Lab users can only retrieve their own laboratory; attempts to read another lab's id return 404 rather than 403 to prevent IDOR enumeration. Platform operators can read any laboratory.

put.summary: Updates a laboratory's configuration and contact fields.
put.description: Lab administrators can update their own laboratory; platform operators can update any. Returns 400 when the laboratory is not found or the caller belongs to a different lab. Active / inactive toggle is included in the request to allow platform operators to deactivate a lab.

requestBody.description: Replacement name, address, contact, and active flag.

get.summary: Returns a page of method configs for the caller's laboratory.
get.description: Soft-deleted configs are excluded. Platform operators without a laboratory claim receive an empty page.

post.summary: Creates a new method config for the caller's laboratory at version 1.
post.description: The laboratory is derived from the `LaboratoryId` JWT claim. Returns 400 when the token has no laboratory claim or when any field value is out of range. The initial snapshot is automatically created alongside the config record. On success the response body contains the new config GUID and the `Location` header points to `GET /api/method-configs/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Regression type, weighting, force-zero flag, label mode, and acceptance thresholds.

get.summary: Returns full method config detail by id, including the current version number.
get.description: Returns 404 when the config does not exist, has been soft-deleted, or belongs to another laboratory. Cross-lab ids are not distinguished from not-found.

put.summary: Updates a method config. Increments the version and appends a snapshot only when a tracked field changed.
put.description: Returns 200 OK with the (possibly unchanged) `currentVersion` in the body — unlike most update endpoints which return 204. This allows callers to see whether a snapshot was created. If no tracked field value changed, the version is not incremented and no new snapshot is appended, making the update idempotent for identical payloads. Returns 409 Conflict on optimistic concurrency failure (stale `RowVersion`). Returns 400 when the config is not found, belongs to another laboratory, or is referenced by an approved calibration group (approved groups are locked).

requestBody.description: Replacement field values for the config.

delete.summary: Soft-deletes a method config. Returns 409 when referenced by any calibration group.
delete.description: Method configs are soft-deleted (excluded from all queries) to preserve audit trail integrity. Returns 409 Conflict when at least one `CalibrationGroup` references this config (regardless of the group's approval status). Reassign or delete those groups first. Returns 400 when the config is not found, already deleted, or belongs to another laboratory.

get.summary: Returns a page of snapshots for a method config, ordered most-recent first.
get.description:
Snapshots are immutable (append-only). Each snapshot captures all config field values at the time
a version was created. Use this endpoint to audit the full configuration history for a calibration group.

Returns an empty page when the config exists but has not been updated since creation
(version 1 snapshot is the creation snapshot).

get.summary: Returns a specific method config snapshot by version number.
get.description: Snapshots are immutable once created. Version numbers start at 1 and increment with each material change. Returns 404 when no snapshot exists at the given version for this config, or when the config itself is not found or belongs to another laboratory.

get.summary: Lists built-in and custom roles visible in the caller's effective laboratory scope.
get.description:
Lab users see built-in roles plus custom roles scoped to their laboratory.
Platform operators without a laboratory claim see all roles across the system.
Platform operators with a `laboratoryId` query parameter see that lab's custom roles plus built-ins.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Platform operators: optional laboratory to scope the list. Lab users: ignored.

post.summary: Creates a custom role in the caller's laboratory.
post.description: Custom roles are scoped to a single laboratory; they cannot be shared across laboratories. The display name must be unique within the laboratory (case-insensitive, after trimming). At least one permission must be provided. All permission values must be from the defined set: `perm.view`, `perm.runs.upload`, `perm.groups.approve`, `perm.config.edit`, `perm.users.manage_lab`, `perm.roles.manage_lab`, `perm.laboratories.manage`, `perm.laboratories.create`, `perm.platform.manage`. Returns 400 when the name is blank, too long (> 128 chars), already taken in the lab, or when any permission value is unknown.

requestBody.description: Display name, optional laboratory id (platform operators), and permission list.

put.summary: Renames a custom role in the caller's laboratory.
put.description: Built-in roles cannot be renamed via this endpoint. The new name must be unique within the laboratory (same normalization and uniqueness rules as create). Returns 400 when the role is not found, is a built-in role, belongs to another laboratory, or the new name collides with an existing custom role.

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: Custom role id (Identity role id, not the display name).

requestBody.description: New display name and optional laboratory id.

put.summary: Replaces all permission claims on a custom role.
put.description: This is a full replace — the provided list becomes the exact set of permissions on the role. Built-in roles cannot be updated via this endpoint. At least one permission must be provided. All values must be valid permission constants. Role/permission changes take effect on the next access-token issuance for affected users. Returns 400 when the role is not found, is built-in, belongs to another laboratory, or any permission value is unrecognized.

requestBody.description: Full replacement permission list and optional laboratory id.

delete.summary: Retires (deletes) a custom role that is not currently assigned to any user.
delete.description: Built-in roles cannot be retired via this endpoint. Returns 400 when the role still has assigned users — remove role assignments first. Returns 400 when the role is not found, is a built-in role, or belongs to another laboratory.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Platform operators: target laboratory id. Lab users: their own lab is used.

post.summary: Assigns one or more roles to a laboratory user.
post.description: Role ids must refer to roles visible to the caller's laboratory (built-in roles or the lab's own custom roles). Cross-lab custom role ids are rejected with 400. Role changes take effect on the next access-token issuance for the target user. The current session is unaffected until the access token expires and is refreshed. Returns 400 when the target user is not found, belongs to a different laboratory, any role id is not found, or any role is outside the laboratory scope.

requestBody.description: Target user id, optional laboratory id (platform operators), and role ids to assign.

delete.summary: Removes one or more roles from a laboratory user.
delete.description: Role changes take effect on the next access-token issuance for the target user. Safety guards — returns 400 when:The caller attempts to remove their own `LabAdmin` role (self-lockout prevention).Removing the `LabAdmin` role would leave the laboratory with no remaining lab admin (orphan-lab prevention).The target user is not found, belongs to a different laboratory, or any role id is invalid.

requestBody.description: Target user id, optional laboratory id (platform operators), and role ids to remove.

get.summary: Returns a filtered page of calibration runs in the caller's laboratory.
get.description: Laboratory scope: lab users see only runs whose instrument belongs to their JWT laboratory. Platform operators see runs across all laboratories. When `instrumentId` is supplied it must belong to the effective scope; an out-of-scope instrument silently returns an empty page. Filters (all optional, combinable): `instrumentId` — restrict to a single instrument.`runType` — case-insensitive: `CAL` or `ICV`.`status` — case-insensitive: `Valid`, `ValidWithWarnings`, or `Invalid`. Sort: `runDate` (default, newest first), `status`, or `createdAt`; append `:asc` or `:desc` to override direction.

parameters.name: instrumentId
parameters.description: Optional: restrict to a single instrument (must be in the effective lab scope).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort expression: `runDate`, `status`, or `createdAt`, optionally suffixed with `:asc` or `:desc`.

post.summary: Uploads a CAL or ICV run from raw instrument text.
post.description: The caller must have an active lab technician profile linked to their Identity user. The `InstrumentId` must belong to the caller's laboratory; cross-lab instrument ids return 400. For `CAL` runs, `Level` is resolved by normalized name to a calibration level in the caller's laboratory. The level name is case-insensitive and ignores underscores, hyphens, and extra whitespace. The response always includes `warnings` (header parse issues), `measurementCount` (number of successfully parsed compound rows), and `measurementWarnings` (flattened human-readable strings: parser warnings, compound-row issues, and structured measurement validation messages such as missing analyte mapping, missing response ratio when IS rows exist, non-positive analyte or IS response, or malformed concentration fields). For machine-readable validation with `code`, `isError`, and `measurementId`, call `GET /api/runs/{id}/validation` after upload. The upload succeeds even when `warnings` or `measurementWarnings` are present — check `status` on the subsequent `GET` detail to determine usability. The `Location` header on the 201 response points to `GET /api/runs/{id}`.

requestBody.description: Run type (CAL/ICV), instrument id, optional calibration level name, acquisition date, and full raw text.

delete.summary: Soft-deletes a run that is not linked to any calibration group.
delete.description: Laboratory users may delete only runs for instruments in their laboratory. If the run is a CAL or ICV member of any group, the request fails with 409 until the run is removed from all groups. Raw text is not returned; the audit trail records a hash and metadata.

get.summary: Returns parsed metadata and status for a single run (excludes raw text).
get.description: 200 — run found and accessible. Platform operators can read any run; lab users only within their laboratory. 404 — no run with this id, related instrument data is missing, or the run belongs to a different laboratory than the caller's (indistinguishable by design to prevent tenancy inference). The response includes `status` (Valid / ValidWithWarnings / Invalid), parsed header fields, and `measurementCount`. Raw text is intentionally excluded from this endpoint; it is available directly in the database for audit purposes.

get.summary: Returns aggregated internal-standard (IS) response statistics for this run (min, max, mean, count per normalized IS name).
get.description: Only measurement rows with InternalStandard compound category are included; statistics use each row's Response (instrument peak area/count), grouped by the same normalized name rules used for IS matching on targets. Laboratory scope: lab users are limited to runs whose instrument belongs to their JWT `LaboratoryId` (otherwise 404). Platform operators must pass `laboratoryId` and it must match that instrument's laboratory — omitting it yields 400; mismatch yields 404. Thresholds: optional `methodConfigId` selects a method config in the effective lab. When present, `internalStandardResponseMin` / `internalStandardResponseMax` on that config (inclusive bounds on the mean) set `isWarning` and `warningMessages` when the mean is strictly outside the configured range. When `methodConfigId` is omitted, no threshold evaluation is performed (statistics only). Export: default response is a JSON array. Use `format=csv` for UTF-8 CSV (`text/csv`) with a header row. See the OpenAPI document introduction (Internal standard response summaries) for the full field reference table.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Required for platform operators; ignored for laboratory users (JWT scope applies).

parameters.name: methodConfigId
parameters.description: Optional method config id for IS mean threshold flags; must belong to the effective laboratory.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format: omit or any value other than `csv` for JSON; `csv` (case-insensitive) for CSV download.

get.summary: Returns compound measurements for a run with analyte resolution and IS normalization fields.
get.description: Measurements are the parsed compound result rows from the run's raw text. Each item includes: `id` — persisted measurement row id.`rawCompoundName` — compound name as parsed from the export.`analyteId` / `canonicalAnalyteName` / `isResolved` — canonical analyte when the name matched an analyte or alias at upload; otherwise null / false.`internalStandardResponse` — response of the matched internal-standard compound row used as the divisor when normalization ran; null when no IS was linked for this row or the analyte has no default IS.`responseRatio` — `analyteResponse / internalStandardResponse` when both the analyte response and IS response are positive and a matching IS row exists; null when normalization did not apply or IS response was not positive (see `GET /api/runs/{id}/validation` for issue codes). Lab scope rules are the same as `GET /api/runs/{id}`:200 — caller is in the run's laboratory (or is a platform operator).404 — no run with this id, related instrument data is missing, or the run belongs to a different laboratory than the caller's (returned as not found for lab users to prevent tenancy inference; same as `GET /api/runs/{id}`).

get.summary: Returns structured validation for parsed measurements (errors versus warnings) and aggregate run status for regression gating.
get.description: Requires `perm.view`. Same laboratory scope as `GET /api/runs/{id}` except cross-lab access returns 403 Forbidden (not masked as 404) for this route. The `status` string mirrors Wltr.Domain.Enums.RunStatus: `Valid`, `ValidWithWarnings`, or `Invalid`. Wltr.Domain.Enums.RunStatus.Invalid means the run must not be used for regression (e.g. no usable target rows). Issues are split into `errors` (`isError: true`) and `warnings` (`isError: false`). Each issue includes `code` (numeric enum), `message`, optional `measurementId`, `rawCompoundName`, `sourceLineNumber`, and optional `calibrationRunId` (set for some group-level readiness findings). Common `code` values (see `RunValidationIssueCode` in schema): `NonPositiveResponse` (0) — target response not positive; `MissingAnalyteMapping` (1); `MissingInternalStandard` (2) — run has IS compound rows but ratio missing for a target row; `MalformedConcentrationOrRatio` (3); `NonPositiveInternalStandardResponse` (4) — IS response zero or negative so ratio cannot be computed; `InsufficientCalibrationLevels` (5) through `InvalidResponseRatio` (10) are used by calibration-group readiness and other aggregate checks.

post.summary: Resolves an unmatched raw compound name to a canonical analyte, with optional permanent alias creation.
post.description: This endpoint handles the analyte-mapping step that follows a run upload where one or more compound rows could not be automatically matched to a canonical analyte. It operates in two distinct modes controlled by `saveAsAlias`: Mode 1 — Save alias (`saveAsAlias: true`): Creates a permanent `AnalyteAlias` in the laboratory that maps `rawCompoundName` to the target `analyteId`. After the alias is persisted, a full laboratory-wide remap runs automatically, resolving every existing unresolved measurement across all runs whose normalized compound name matches the alias. The `applyScope` field has no effect in this mode. If an alias for this normalized name already exists and points to the same analyte, no duplicate is created; a lab remap still runs and `createdAliasId` will be `null`.If an alias already exists pointing to a different analyte, the request is rejected with 400. Mode 2 — Direct assignment (`saveAsAlias: false`): Assigns the target analyte directly to matching unresolved measurements without persisting any alias. No future imports are affected. `applyScope` controls which measurements are updated: `RunOnly` (default) — only unresolved measurements in this run whose normalized compound name matches.`Laboratory` — all unresolved measurements in the entire laboratory (across all runs) whose normalized compound name matches.`createdAliasId` is always `null` in this mode. Name matching: `rawCompoundName` is normalized before matching — comparison is case-insensitive and ignores hyphens, underscores, and extra whitespace. Only measurements that are currently unresolved (no `analyteId` assigned) are candidates; already-resolved rows are skipped. Scope enforcement: The run must belong to the caller's laboratory. The target analyte must also belong to the same laboratory. Cross-lab run IDs return 404. Response: `updatedMeasurementCount` reflects the number of measurement rows whose `analyteId` was changed (may be 0 if no unresolved rows matched). `createdAliasId` is the new alias row ID when one was created, otherwise `null`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Run identifier — must belong to the caller's laboratory.

requestBody.description:
`rawCompoundName` — compound text as it appears on measurement rows (normalized for matching).
`analyteId` — target canonical analyte in the run's laboratory.
`saveAsAlias` — when `true`, persists a lab alias and triggers a full-lab remap (ignores `applyScope`).
`applyScope` — `RunOnly` or `Laboratory`; only used when `saveAsAlias` is `false`.

get.summary: Lists users in the caller's effective scope.
get.description:
Requires `perm.users.manage_lab`. Platform operators can list all users,
or pass `laboratoryId` to scope the list to one laboratory.
Each item includes identity `firstName`, `lastName`, and `laboratoryName` when available.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: When platform operator, optional filter to one laboratory.

get.summary: Returns user administration detail including assigned roles for a single Identity user.
get.description: Requires `perm.users.manage_lab`. Lab admins are scoped to their JWT laboratory. Platform operators may pass optional `laboratoryId` to assert membership; when omitted, any user id may be loaded. Returns 404 when the user does not exist or is outside the caller's scope (IDOR-safe). Role assignment changes still require `perm.roles.manage_lab` on `/api/Roles/assignments`.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Platform operators: optional laboratory membership assert.

put.summary: Deactivates a lab user account, immediately blocking login and revoking all refresh tokens.
put.description: Sets Identity lockout to `DateTimeOffset.MaxValue`, marks the linked `LabTechnician` as inactive, and revokes all active refresh tokens for the user. The user's current access token continues to work until it expires naturally (up to the configured token lifetime), but no new sessions can be established. Returns 400 when: The caller attempts to deactivate themselves (self-lockout prevention).A platform operator omits the required `laboratoryId` query parameter.The user is not found in the specified laboratory.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Required for platform operators; specifies the target laboratory. Lab admins use their own lab automatically.

put.summary: Reactivates a previously deactivated lab user account, restoring login access.
put.description: Clears the Identity lockout end date and restores the linked `LabTechnician` to active. The user must obtain a fresh access token via `POST /api/Auth/login` — existing refresh tokens were revoked at deactivation time and cannot be reused. Returns 400 when: A platform operator omits the required `laboratoryId` query parameter.The user is not found in the specified laboratory.

parameters.name: laboratoryId
parameters.description: Required for platform operators; specifies the target laboratory. Lab admins use their own lab automatically.

AnalyteAliasItemDto.description: Alias mapping for a canonical analyte.

AnalyteDetailDto.description: Canonical analyte with its alias mappings.

AnalyteListItemDto.description: Summary row for laboratory analyte list views.

AnalyteMappingApplyScope.description: Scope for applying a one-off analyte assignment when not saving an alias.

name.description: Display name.

score.description: Higher means a closer match (implementation-defined scale).

AnalyteSuggestionItemDto.description: One ranked canonical analyte candidate for fuzzy matching raw compound text.

token.description: Raw one-time invitation token provided to the invitee.

currentPassword.description: The user's current password for verification.

email.description: Email address of the user account.

accessToken.description: JWT access token for bearer authentication.

refreshToken.description: Opaque refresh token used to rotate the session.

accessTokenExpiresAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the access token expires.

laboratoryId.description: User laboratory id; null for platform users without lab scope.

rowVersion.description: Concurrency token for the technician profile; null when no technician profile exists.

refreshToken.description: Opaque refresh token previously issued by the API.

userId.description: Identity user id from the one-time reset link.

token.description: One-time reset token from the reset link.

firstName.description: First name for the linked technician profile.

lastName.description: Last name for the linked technician profile.

rowVersion.description: Concurrency token from the last GET me response.

data.description: Array of Plotly.js trace objects. Up to three traces are present: Calibration Points (included scatter), Excluded Points (excluded scatter), and Fitted Curve (line evaluated from the regression equation at 100 evenly-spaced X values). The fitted curve is omitted for the `Average` regression type or when fewer than two included points exist.

layout.description: Plotly.js layout object. Includes axis titles (True Concentration for X, Response Ratio for Y) and default legend settings.

config.description: Plotly.js config object containing rendering options.

CalibrationCurveChartResponse.description:
Plotly.js figure specification returned by `GET /api/calibration-groups/{id}/analytes/{analyteId}/chart`.
Pass Wltr.Api.Models.CalibrationCurveChartResponse.Data and Wltr.Api.Models.CalibrationCurveChartResponse.Layout directly to `Plotly.react(element, data, layout)`

weight.description: Regression weight applied at fit time; 0 for excluded points.

forcedZero.description: `true` when the intercept was forced to zero.

s0.description: Σ w — sum of weights over included points.

denominator.description: Normal-equation denominator (S0·Sxx − Sx² for linear; matrix determinant for quadratic).

quadraticCoefficient.description: Quadratic term a; `null` for non-quadratic models.

sse.description: Weighted sum of squared residuals over included points.

sst.description: Weighted total sum of squares over included points.

rse.description: Residual standard error √(SSE / (n − p)); `null` when degrees of freedom ≤ 0 (degenerate fit — too few points for the model).

correlationR.description: Pearson r (linear models) or correlation of observed vs. predicted (quadratic); `null` when not computed.

fitQualityValue.description:
Single value the report should display for fit quality: Wltr.Api.Models.RegressionDebugResponse.RSquared when
Wltr.Api.Models.RegressionDebugResponse.LabelMode is `RSquared`, otherwise `√R²` (clamped at zero to absorb
floating-point drift).

includedPointCount.description: Number of points included in the regression fit.

excludedPointCount.description: Number of points excluded from the regression fit.

failureReasons.description: Human-readable failure descriptions when Wltr.Api.Models.RegressionDebugResponse.CalStatus is `Fail`; `null` when passing or not yet evaluated.

icvTrueConcentration.description: Known ICV standard concentration; `null` when no ICV run was linked.

icvCalculatedConcentration.description: ICV concentration derived by inverting the fitted curve; `null` when no ICV run was linked.

icvPercentDiff.description: ICV %Diff = (Calc − True) / True × 100; `null` when no ICV run was linked.

icvPassed.description: `true` when the ICV %Diff is within the configured limit; `null` when no ICV run was linked.

Fitted ŷ at this point's X from the calibration curve equation; `null` when the group has not been computed
or the model does not produce a forward prediction (e.g. Average).

residual.description: Observed Y minus fitted ŷ; `null` when Wltr.Api.Models.RegressionInputPointResponse.PredictedY is `null`.

percentDiff.description:
`(CalcConc − TrueConc) / TrueConc × 100` where `CalcConc` is obtained by inverting the fitted
            curve for the observed response; `null` when inversion is undefined or the group has not been computed.

WeightingMode.description: **Weighted least-squares** mode for calibration curves. JSON integers: **0** = None (w=1 for each included point), **1** = InverseX (w=1/X), **2** = InverseXSquared (w=1/X²). Inverse modes require every **included** point to have curve X > 0 at compute. Excluded points get weight 0. Full narrative: OpenAPI description section **Calibration regression and weighting**.

Bearer.description: Obtain a JWT from **POST /api/Auth/login** (or refresh via **POST /api/Auth/refresh**). In Swagger UI, click **Authorize**, choose **Bearer**, and paste **only** the raw access token string (the UI adds the `Bearer ` prefix). Most endpoints require a valid token; anonymous routes are login, refresh, forgot-password, reset-password, and accept-invite.
